sources: NextStep field (updated 1 Jun 2026): '5k active suppliers, 30-40k inactive suppliers'
Demo call 21 Oct 2025: supplier scale and onboarding complexity discussed
NextStep: 'email sent by Mona with ROI Business Case questions to further qualify' (no reply logged)
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated a supplier-scale pain on the 21 Oct 2025 demo: 5,000 active suppliers and 30,000 to 40,000 inactive or one-time suppliers currently not managed in their data (per NextStep summary and demo context). The pain is operational complexity and lack of visibility across a fragmented supplier base spanning Macedonia, Bulgaria, and Austria for S2C. No quantified financial impact (cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI) was stated on the call or logged in any Salesforce field. The NextStep references a follow-up ROI email with Business Case questions sent by the rep to further qualify, but no buyer reply or quantified metrics have been captured. The business problem is confirmed (supplier data fragmentation, scale); the quantified value case remains a gap and is the subject of an unanswered outreach.

sources: Demo call 21 Oct 2025 attendees: wolfgang.foerster@evn.at, oliver.augustin@evn.at (evaluators, no advocacy evidence)
OpportunityContactRole: Alexander Fuhri (Buyer, Evaluator), Mario Greile (Procurement Lead, Evaluator)
8 months of silence since demo (21 Oct 2025 to 26 Jun 2026)
Demo call action item: 'consult with the department head to confirm which EVN team members will attend the Zycus Horizon event' (no follow-up logged)
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. The 21 Oct 2025 demo was attended by the relevant stakeholder (Procurement Manager) and Oliver Augustin (strategic procurement expert), both operational evaluators, but neither has demonstrated advocacy, provided internal intel, or driven the process forward. The deal has been silent for 8 months since that call, with no follow-up, no Horizon event attendance (the invitation was extended on the call but no confirmation logged), and no reply to the ROI Business Case email referenced in NextStep. The two buyer-side contact roles (Alexander Fuhri, Mario Greile) are also evaluators with no champion behaviors on record. No coach or internal sponsor has been named. The silence suggests either no champion exists or the champion has deprioritized Zycus due to the SAP rollout. Building a champion who can navigate the internal project-definition and RFP-timing uncertainty is the top priority.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source (Salesforce Competitors__c field is unknown/empty, no mention in the 21 Oct 2025 demo notes or transcript, and NextStep carries no competitive intel). The buyer stated on the call that they are 'building a shortlist for future RFPs', which implies a multi-vendor evaluation, but no other vendors have been identified. The competitive landscape is genuinely unknown. For a deal this cold and this far from an RFP, the competitive read cannot be reconstructed; the first re-engagement conversation must discover who else is on the shortlist and what the evaluation timeline is.

sources: NextStep (1 Jun 2026): '5k active suppliers, 30-40k inactive suppliers currently no in data. Macedonia, Bulgaria and Austria for S2C in scope'
Demo call 21 Oct 2025 attendees: wolfgang.foerster@evn.at (Procurement Manager), oliver.augustin@evn.at (strategic procurement)
Demo call 21 Oct 2025, buyer speaker 1: 'EVN has been mid-rollout of SAP S/4HANA since October 1, constraining available workshop time'
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is supplier data fragmentation and lack of a unified platform for managing 5,000 active suppliers and 30,000 to 40,000 inactive or one-time suppliers across three geographies (Macedonia, Bulgaria, Austria) within the S2C scope. The buyer is currently operating without a centralized supplier management system ('currently no in data' per NextStep), creating operational complexity, risk-management gaps, and reporting blind spots. The pain owner is the procurement function, represented on the 21 Oct 2025 demo by the relevant stakeholder (Procurement Manager) and Oliver Augustin (strategic procurement expert). The SAP S/4HANA rollout (live since 1 Oct 2025) is an adjacent constraint, limiting bandwidth for new-tool workshops and likely forcing a phased approach (Procurement module first, Contract later). The pain is operationally confirmed but not yet quantified in financial or cycle-time terms.

sources: NextStep (1 Jun 2026): 'Contract Q3 of 2027 if provider is not ready'
Demo call 21 Oct 2025: no legal, InfoSec, or contracting discussion in notes or transcript
Demo call 21 Oct 2025 action item: buyer to 'consult with the department head' for event attendance (informal coordination)
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or procurement mechanics have been discussed. The NextStep references a Contract phase in Q3 2027 (if provider ready), but no MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review, or InfoSec requirements have been raised on the call or in Salesforce fields. No SI or partner involvement is mentioned. The 21 Oct 2025 demo notes include an action item for the buyer to 'consult with the department head to confirm which EVN team members will attend the Zycus Horizon event and handle ticket purchases', suggesting internal coordination is informal and no formal procurement channel has been opened. The deal is pre-RFP, so paper-process definition is a future step, contingent on the buyer confirming project scope and entering the RFP stage.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Alexander Fuhri (Buyer, Evaluator), Mario Greile (Procurement Lead, Evaluator)

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. The 21 Oct 2025 demo was attended by oliver.augustin@evn.at, wolfgang.foerster@evn.at (Procurement Manager per account contacts), and two unidentified EVN addresses (vico10-at-di-hg@evn.at, res295@evn.at). No C-level or CFO participation. The two buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity are Alexander Fuhri (Buyer, Evaluator) and Mario Greile (Procurement Lead, Evaluator), neither of which signals budget authority. No Salesforce field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, or equivalent) names an EB, and no call or NextStep references executive sponsorship or budget sign-off. The deal is 8 months cold, so no recent evidence exists to map the EB. Identifying and engaging the CFO or procurement executive sponsor is the critical next step.

sources: Demo call 21 Oct 2025, buyer speaker 1: 'EVN is not actively seeking a new tool immediately but building a shortlist for future RFPs'
Demo call 21 Oct 2025, buyer speaker 1: 'formal RFP participation and budget discussions to follow once project scope and user numbers are confirmed'
Demo call 21 Oct 2025, buyer speaker 1: 'EVN has been mid-rollout of SAP S/4HANA since October 1, constraining available workshop time'
NextStep (1 Jun 2026): phased plan (Procurement autumn 2026, Contract Q3 2027)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer stated on the 21 Oct 2025 call that they are 'not actively seeking a new tool immediately but building a shortlist for future RFPs' and that 'formal RFP participation and budget discussions to follow once project scope and user numbers are confirmed'. The phased rollout plan in NextStep (Procurement module by autumn 2026, then Contract Q3 2027 if provider ready; otherwise iContract bundled from start) suggests a two-stage decision: first select the Procurement/Supplier Management platform, then decide whether to add Contract. The 21 Oct 2025 demo was a follow-up session, implying an earlier discovery or intro call occurred (not on record). EVN has been mid-rollout of SAP S/4HANA since 1 Oct 2025 (per call), which constrained workshop availability and likely delays the formal RFP. No approval chain, committee structure, or sign-off milestones have been documented. The call sequence (intro → demo → stall) and the 8-month silence suggest the process stalled after the demo, likely due to SAP rollout prioritization and lack of internal project definition (scope, user count).

sources: Demo call 21 Oct 2025: agenda covered onboarding, risk, reporting, sourcing (Avoma notes)
Demo call 21 Oct 2025, buyer speaker 1: 'barrier-free supplier onboarding to grant event access' (high-priority requirement)
NextStep (1 Jun 2026): 'Using lean request by SAP. Procurement module by autumn 2026 then Contract Q3 of 2027'

Demo call 21 Oct 2025, buyer speaker 1: 'Zycus will remain on the potential tool short-list; formal RFP participation and budget discussions to follow once project scope and user numbers are confirmed'
decision_criteria.narrative: The 21 Oct 2025 demo covered supplier self-onboarding (customizable registration, dynamic fields, approval workflows), AI-driven risk management (external data integration, scorecards, KPIs, alerts), integrated reporting via the relevant stakeholder, and sourcing negotiation support (multi-round price comparisons, upcoming advanced module). The buyer's implicit criteria from the demo agenda are: ease of supplier onboarding, risk visibility, reporting flexibility without external BI, and negotiation transparency. One explicit high-priority criterion was stated: 'barrier-free supplier onboarding to grant event access' (per notes). The NextStep mentions 'Using lean request by the relevant stakeholder module by autumn 2026 then Contract Q3 of 2027 if provider is not ready; otherwise iContract is part of it from the get go', suggesting SAP S/4HANA integration is a constraint and phasing (Procurement first, Contract second unless bundled) is part of the decision logic. No formal RFP criteria, scorecard, or written BRD has been shared. The buyer confirmed Zycus remains on the shortlist pending project scope and user-number confirmation (per demo close), but the formal criteria for down-select are unconfirmed.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS score on record. The 21 Oct 2025 demo covered AI-driven risk management, and the buyer engaged with it as a capability (no objection, no excitement). The lack of AI in the buyer's stated decision criteria, RFP timing, or NextStep phasing suggests the buyer is AI Curious (aware of AI, open to it as a feature, but not driving the purchase decision around it). The deal is too cold to refine this read further.

deal_movement.summary: Created 22 May 2026, last modified 1 Jun 2026 (NextStep update). The only buyer touchpoint was a follow-up demo on 21 Oct 2025 (8 months ago, pre-dates opp creation by 7 months, likely linked retroactively or this is a data-integrity anomaly). No movement in 2026. The NextStep references a rep-initiated ROI email and a demo plan but carries no buyer reply or engagement date. The deal is cold.

items.detail: The close date (5 May 2027, 313 days out) is a placeholder and was never validated by the buyer. The buyer's stated timeline (Procurement module by autumn 2026, Contract Q3 2027) is 3 to 12 months earlier than the Salesforce close date, but the deal has been dark for 8 months and the RFP timing is unconfirmed. The SAP S/4HANA rollout (live since 1 Oct 2025) is a known constraint that delayed the evaluation and may continue to push the RFP window.

No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or C-level sponsor has been identified or engaged. The 21 Oct 2025 demo was operational-level only. No budget authority, no executive sponsorship, no sign-off path is mapped. This is a blocking vulnerability for any RFP or contract stage.

8 months of silence with no champion, no EB, and no reply to the ROI email. The deal may have been shelved or deprioritized due to the SAP rollout. The risk is that Zycus has fallen off the buyer's radar and will not be included in the RFP when it opens.

The NextStep references 'Using lean request by SAP', implying SAP S/4HANA integration is a requirement. No integration scoping, API discussion, or technical validation has occurred. This is a future risk if the RFP advances.

The deal has been silent for 8 months with no follow-up, no reply to the ROI email, and no Horizon event engagement. No coach or internal sponsor has been named. Champion-building is the top priority alongside EB mapping.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cold: 8 months since the last buyer conversation (21 Oct 2025), no reply to the ROI email, no Horizon event follow-up, no champion, no EB, and no verified activity in the 2026 window. The confidence_signals are all negative. The deal is at high risk of being shelved or lost to a competitor on the undisclosed shortlist.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 22 May 2026, qualified same window, close 5 May 2027 (313 days out). Last buyer touchpoint was a follow-up demo on 21 Oct 2025 (8 months ago, ~248 days). Zero verified activity since then per LastActivityDate (none on record). Time in Qualified stage is at least 25 days (last modified 1 Jun 2026). The NextStep references a ROI email from Mona and mentions BME Düsseldorf and a demo plan, but no date or buyer confirmation is logged, and the 1 Jun last-modified date suggests this was a rep-side update, not a buyer reply. The deal has been cold for 8 months; the close date is a placeholder and cannot be defended.

No champion, no EB, no DM mapped; 2 buyer-side contact roles (Alexander Fuhri, Mario Greile) but no recent contact
Stage Qualified with 313 days to close; no submission-date series to anchor stage velocity
Products in scope: iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement; buyer scale 5k active suppliers, 30-40k inactive
Buyer timeline from NextStep: Procurement module by autumn 2026, Contract Q3 2027 if provider ready; otherwise iContract included from start
Zero calls, tasks, or field-history moves in 2026 window
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dormant nine months after last buyer conversation (21 Oct 2025). No activity in 2026, no champion or EB mapped, and buyer is still in pre-RFP shortlist phase with zero re-engagement evidence since qualification.

flags: Single-thread risk: only 2 buyer-side contact roles (Alexander Fuhri, Mario Greile) and 2 attendees on the 21 Oct 2025 demo (Wolfgang Foerster, Oliver Augustin), all operational evaluators. No C-level, CFO, or procurement executive mapped. Multi-thread to the EB and a senior procurement sponsor before the RFP window opens.
best_practice_check.summary: This deal is 8 months dark and has critical multi-thread, champion, and EB gaps. The SAP S/4HANA rollout (live since 1 Oct 2025) likely stalled the evaluation, but the silence is now long enough to risk disqualification. The immediate need is a surgical re-engagement that acknowledges the SAP constraint, offers a low-touch value anchor (a peer reference or a pilot-light check-in), and discovers the current shortlist, RFP timing, and internal project status. The ranked plays below reflect this: re-engage first, then map the EB and build a champion in parallel.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor named in any source. The buyer stated on 21 Oct 2025 that they are 'building a shortlist for future RFPs', implying a multi-vendor evaluation, but no other vendors have been disclosed. The competitive landscape is unknown. For an 8-month-cold deal pre-RFP, the competitive read must be discovered on re-engagement.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was demonstrated on the 21 Oct 2025 call (AI-driven risk management with external data integration, scorecards, alerts) and was well-received in the context of risk visibility. No AIS score is on record, and the buyer has not articulated AI as a strategic priority or a decision criterion. The NextStep references a phased rollout (Procurement first, Contract later) with no mention of AI or Merlin. AI is a feature the buyer saw and understood but not a strategic lever in this deal. No under-positioning risk is evident; the bigger risk is that the deal is dark and the AI story has not been reinforced in 8 months.

value.detail: The close date (5 May 2027, 313 days out) is a placeholder and was never validated by the buyer. The buyer's stated timeline (Procurement module by autumn 2026, Contract Q3 2027) is 3 to 12 months earlier than the Salesforce close date, but the deal has been dark for 8 months and the RFP timing is unconfirmed. The SAP S/4HANA rollout (live since 1 Oct 2025) is a known constraint that delayed the evaluation and may continue to push the RFP window.
value.source: Demo call 21 Oct 2025 (SAP rollout constraint), NextStep (phased timeline), Salesforce CloseDate

packets.subject: The close date (5 May 2027, 313 days out) is a placeholder and was never validated by the buyer. The buyer's stated timeline (Procurement module by autumn 2026, Contract Q3 2027) is 3 to 12 months earlier than the Salesforce close date, but the deal has been dark for 8 months and the RFP timing is unconfirmed. The SAP S/4HANA rollout (live since 1 Oct 2025) is a known constraint that delayed the evaluation and may continue to push the RFP window.

value.detail: No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or C-level sponsor has been identified or engaged. The 21 Oct 2025 demo was operational-level only. No budget authority, no executive sponsorship, no sign-off path is mapped. This is a blocking vulnerability for any RFP or contract stage.
value.source: OpportunityContactRole, demo call 21 Oct 2025, Salesforce fields

packets.subject: No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or C-level sponsor has been identified or engaged. The 21 Oct 2025 demo was operational-level only. No budget authority, no executive sponsorship, no sign-off path is mapped. This is a blocking vulnerability for any RFP or contract stage.

value.detail: 8 months of silence with no champion, no EB, and no reply to the ROI email. The deal may have been shelved or deprioritized due to the SAP rollout. The risk is that Zycus has fallen off the buyer's radar and will not be included in the RFP when it opens.
value.source: Deal movement, demo call 21 Oct 2025, NextStep

packets.subject: 8 months of silence with no champion, no EB, and no reply to the ROI email. The deal may have been shelved or deprioritized due to the SAP rollout. The risk is that Zycus has fallen off the buyer's radar and will not be included in the RFP when it opens.

value.detail: The NextStep references 'Using lean request by SAP', implying SAP S/4HANA integration is a requirement. No integration scoping, API discussion, or technical validation has occurred. This is a future risk if the RFP advances.

packets.subject: The NextStep references 'Using lean request by SAP', implying SAP S/4HANA integration is a requirement. No integration scoping, API discussion, or technical validation has occurred. This is a future risk if the RFP advances.

value.flag: Single-thread risk: only 2 buyer-side contact roles (Alexander Fuhri, Mario Greile) and 2 attendees on the 21 Oct 2025 demo (Wolfgang Foerster, Oliver Augustin), all operational evaluators. No C-level, CFO, or procurement executive mapped. Multi-thread to the EB and a senior procurement sponsor before the RFP window opens.

gaps: Only 1 buyer call on record (21 Oct 2025, 8 months ago); no 2026 engagement.
No Salesforce tasks, events, or field-history moves in the 2026 window to reconstruct deal activity.
LastActivityDate is none (no verified activity timestamp).
No AIS score, no MEDDPICC boolean fields, no custom-field synthesis-map data (Q3 query likely failed or fields do not exist in this org).
No submission-date series (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, etc.) to compute true time-in-stage.
Buyer-side contact roles are thin (2 only: Alexander Fuhri, Mario Greile); no EB, DM, or Champion role on record.
No competitor, no RFP timeline, no approval chain documented in any source.
The 21 Oct 2025 call pre-dates the opp creation date (22 May 2026) by 7 months, suggesting either retroactive association or a data-integrity anomaly.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Wolfgang Foerster')
